new_classes.label_en: Waters, incl. mineral waters and aerated waters, containing added sugar or other sweetening matter or flavoured (subheading 2202 10)
new_classes.label_de: Wasser, einschl. Mineralwasser und kohlensäurehaltiges Wasser, mit Zusatz von Zucker, anderen Süßmitteln oder Aromastoffen (Unterposition 2202 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Water, including mineral water and aerated (carbonated) water, to which sugar, other sweetening matter or flavouring has been added, classified under CN subheading 2202 10. Covers soft drinks as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2202, paragraph (A). The presence of anti-oxidants, vitamins, stabilisers or quinine does not affect classification.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 2202 10 are: (1) the base substance is water (including mineral water and aerated/carbonated water), (2) it contains added sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring, and (3) it is non-alcoholic. These are all captured via the SweetenedOrFlavouredQuality and NonAlcoholicBeverageQuality restrictions on the new subclass of FlavoredOrSweetenedWater. The legal note further clarifies that the presence of anti-oxidants, vitamins, stabilisers or quinine does not affect classification — this is a negative/permissive rule that cannot be directly expressed as an OWL restriction and is noted in the definition text only. The distinction from heading 2202 other non-alcoholic beverages (sibling 2202 90) is partially captured by placing this class under FlavoredOrSweetenedWater (water base) rather than the broader NonAlcoholicFlavouredBeverage2202; however the TBox does not currently have an explicit 'WaterBase' quality class to make this crisp, reducing coverage slightly. The complement restriction was removed from the final proposal as it would cause a logical inconsistency (the new class IS a subclass of FlavoredOrSweetenedWater which is a sibling concept, not a complement). Coverage is estimated at 0.82 due to inability to formally encode the anti-oxidant/vitamin/quinine permissive rule and the lack of a dedicated water-base quality.
